authority_boundary.allowed_claim: Public provider context recipes can describe bounded byte budgets, ordered section fill, omitted-section manifests, graph roles, and reducer deliverable types.
forbidden_claims: proof bodies or oracle-only truth-side material may enter public context
Lean/Lake or Mathlib-dependent proof execution is authorized
release, publication, or recipient work is authorized

validation_rules: Every recipe budget must match the public recipe table ceiling.
Recipes must fill sections in order and emit omitted-section manifests when material is excluded by the budget.
Truth-side, oracle-only, provider body, and proof body fields are forbidden.
Provider call authority must stay false.
Every recipe deliverable type must match its reducer route contract.
The provider-context source module manifest must carry exact copied macro source bodies for the recipe compiler and formal ladder evaluator.
Copied source module digests must match source-to-target and retain required provider-context anchors.
Release, provider, proof-authority, and Lean/Lake authority overclaims are rejected.
receipt_expectations: result receipt with recipe projections and omitted-section metadata
source module import receipt fields with paths, digests, anchor results, and no copied source body text
board receipt with public contract and authority ceiling
anti_claim: This standard governs public provider context recipe budgets and exact copied non-secret source modules only; it does not call providers, expose proof bodies, certify theorem correctness, authorize Lean/Lake, authorize release, or prove private macro equivalence.
known_failure_modes: provider-context macro source is named only as a source_ref while no copied target module exists inside Microcosm
copied provider-context source module digest diverges from the macro source digest
provider context recipe compiler anchors are absent from the copied graph benchmark source
formal ladder transform-job or reduction anchors are absent from the copied ladder evaluator source
truth-side sections, proof bodies, oracle-needed premise ids, or provider payload bodies enter public context packets
provider call authority is smuggled into a public recipe or source-module receipt
omitted over-budget sections are hidden instead of emitted through an omitted_sections manifest
recipe deliverable type drifts from the reducer route contract
budget validation claims theorem proof, Lean/Lake execution, release, publication, or private macro equivalence

standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe provider-context recipe budget organ over copied macro source modules plus redacted public recipe metadata.
standard_payload.teleology: Make the real provider-context recipe compiler and formal ladder consumer inspectable in Microcosm while keeping provider payload bodies, proof bodies, live access state, credentials, and release authority excluded.
standard_payload.anti_purpose: Turning copied recipe-policy source or metadata into provider-call authority, proof authority, private-root equivalence, hosted publication, or release authority.
failure_modes: provider-context macro source is named only as a source_ref while no copied target module exists inside Microcosm
copied provider-context source module digest diverges from the macro source digest
provider context recipe compiler anchors are absent from the copied graph benchmark source
formal ladder transform-job or reduction anchors are absent from the copied ladder evaluator source
truth-side sections, proof bodies, oracle-needed premise ids, or provider payload bodies enter public context packets
provider call authority is smuggled into a public recipe or source-module receipt
omitted over-budget sections are hidden instead of emitted through an omitted_sections manifest
recipe deliverable type drifts from the reducer route contract
budget validation claims theorem proof, Lean/Lake execution, release, publication, or private macro equivalence
standard_payload.rebuild_action: run provider_context_recipe_budget_policy on fixture and exported bundle, then refresh macro projection import accounting and workingness
